options.label: B
options.text: The extension of a spring is directly proportional to the force applied, up to the elastic limit

options.label: C
options.text: Springs can only be compressed, not stretched

options.label: D
options.text: The more you stretch a spring, the less force is needed

explanation: Hooke's Law tells us that if you double the force on a spring, it stretches twice as much (within limits). This relationship is: Force = spring constant × extension.
